title: DARP: Difference-Aware Retrieval Policies for Imitation Learning
summary: DARP is a semi-parametric, retrieval-based imitation-learning method that reparameterizes the problem in terms of local neighborhood structure: it predicts actions from the k-nearest expert demonstrations, their actions, and the difference vectors between neighbor and query states. It needs no extra data collection, online expert feedback, or task-specific knowledge, and yields consistent 15–46% improvements over standard behavior cloning across diverse domains. On FurnitureBench it improves real-robot success 2.1x (44.0% to 92.0%) and is compatible with diffusion policies.
